title: Dense int8 GEMM tile PPA
hypothesis: A dense signed-int8 GEMM tile can provide materially higher MAC/cycle/mm2 than the exact-FP16 dense tile, which may close the Llama7B mixed-precision dual-stream compute-area gap when Q8/K8/V6 quality gates are used.

direct_comparison.primary_question: What area, power, and delay do 64-MAC and 128-MAC signed-int8 dense GEMM tiles achieve on Nangate45, and how does their density compare to the FP16 dense GEMM tile anchor?
include: same macro-style narrow self-stimulating hash harness as the FP16 dense tile
structural guard that all MAC outputs fold into the visible result hash
exclude: do not claim model quality from this PPA item
do not include softmax, normalization, reducer, SRAM, NoC, or producer integration
do not replace the FP16 compute model until a follow-up L2 substitution consumes the measured int8 rows

expected_benefit: turns the quality-backed Q8/K8 attention compute precision into measured dense-array PPA
tests whether the mixed-precision area gap is mostly due to using FP16 dense compute metrics
provides explicit source-identified int8 compute candidates for follow-up L2 schedule substitution
risks: int8 accumulator width and reduction strategy are still local tile choices, not a complete attention datapath
the wrapper measures dense compute only and omits surrounding SRAM/NoC integration
area savings may be reduced by routing, accumulator width, or hierarchy overhead

required_evaluations.objective: Measure Nangate45 PPA for 8x8 and 16x8 signed-int8 dense GEMM tiles with hierarchy preserved.

expected_result.reason: Use measured int8 dense compute PPA to decide whether mixed-precision dual-stream Llama7B attention becomes physically feasible.
